title: Unnoticed by Anyone, in the Place of Which Everyone Dreams
overview: Ono Goro, a young man with few friends and poor mental health, is appalled to find out he's not hallucinating when he's informed that he's acquired superhuman abilities and must battle others in a death game.

title: Is He Strong, Is He Weak?
overview: Goro used his new karma-twisting powers to resurrect Honoka. The two of them form an alliance along with his friend and fellow God candidate Akitsu, and Honoka provides them with an explanation of some of the details of their new status as candidates.

title: You're the Finest of Friends
overview: Despite forming an alliance, Lall warns Goro to remain wary of Akitsu and Honoka. But Goro trusts his good friend Akitsu, in particular, and even when he steals his smartphone, Goro writes it off as just a prank. But when he finds him at their arcade...

title: What That Means Is, We're Kindred Spirits
overview: Honoka tells a depressed Goro about Little Angel, who can resolve any problem a person might have. Chika from the Science Club joins in the hunt, and they discover that Tatsuya is Little Angel. But Tatsuya is already the target of Koki the delinquent...

title: So Upright and Strong
overview: After a late-night SOS call from Chika, Goro heads to school. There he finds Koki fighting off a bunch of students wearing strange masks. Upon figuring out that "Little Angel" Tatsuya is a God candidate, Goro sets out with Koki and Chika to stop him.

title: Form, Voice, Memories, and Even the Pain
overview: Goro accidentally bought two tickets for his favorite idol Iyo's concert, so he invites Koki along. After learning that Koki has become a KamiErabi God candidate in Tatsuya's place, Goro ends up running into Iyo in a men's bathroom just prior to the show.

title: Close Your Eyes, Cover Your Ears, and Afterwards
overview: Honoka attempts to murder Goro, who's currently incapacitated due to the repeated use of his power. But after Koki and Lall hinder her, a frustrated Honoka encounters Chika while attempting to leave the hospital. Events then take a turn for the bizarre.

title: Introductions Will Have to Wait
overview: During Goro's recovery celebration at the arcade, sexy actress Mitsuko suddenly climbs out of the TV. She asks for help rescuing her friend from a meddling God candidate named Keita. Meanwhile, Honoka tries to get info about other candidates from Chika.

title: If You Took a Trip, Where Would You Want to Go?
overview: Falsely branded as fugitives, Goro's group forms a new alliance with Honoka and Chika. They use Mitsuko's power to leap through space and escape Keita's attack, but a navigational blunder on Mitsuko's part leads them to a surprise acquaintance of Keita's.

title: It's Not Rain That's Falling
overview: Honoka, Koki, and Chika find themselves forced into a harrowing battle against Kyo. Goro and Iyo begin to question their own powers and the true significance of the KamiErabi. Iyo presses Lall for information, but she claims to not remember anything.

title: The Light Hasn't Reached Us Yet
overview: Kyo died young before a treatment could be found for his disease, but Ryo wished him back into being when she became a God candidate. Now Kyo has full control of the parallel worlds Ryo creates, and he asks Goro to help him establish a utopia for her.

title: My Wish Belongs to Me
overview: Goro succeeds in stopping Kyo and Ryo, but the use of his power robs him of the relationships he's established with his fellow God candidates. Lall alone remains by his side, and Goro confronts his own past in order to recall his long-forgotten true wish.

title: That's Why It Needed God
Finally, My Purpose in Life
overview: Lall remembers Goro, but he appears to have been erased from nearly everyone else's memories. Ryo suggests to Eko that she befriend Lall and help her navigate the upcoming KamiErabi battles, an idea to which Eko reluctantly agrees.

title: Unless You Put Your Sorrow behind You, Happiness Is Just an Illusion
overview: Lall's next KamiErabi match is against Honoka, but Honoka has apparently had a change of heart during the past twelve years. She might not even be willing to fight unless she's offered a compelling reason.

title: You're the Last Person I Want to Let Become God
overview: Lall is on a mission to resurrect Goro so he can win the KamiErabi and become God. She and Eko seek out Iyo, hoping her powers might prove useful. But Iyo has a lot going on in her own life at the moment, so will she be able to help?

title: The World Was Already Messed Up
So Don't Cry, It'll Be Okay
If You Want to Save the World, Just Tap That Smartphone
I Thought Up My Own Lines
I Mean, of Course, It's... Me
Everything Turned Out Like This Because You Wanted It To
Even If Means Ruining This Story
The Next World You Write Will Be
